rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE LAKISHA WILSON TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Allowance Of Attorneys Fees And Costs
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing but with reduction of attorney costs by $15 for unnecessary costs to e-file notice of reset hearing [Probate Code Section 1205], especially in pleading form and not utilizing the mandatory Judicial Council form DE-120, and a duplicate e-filing cost for the Substitution of Attorney. The costs should have been further reduced for the filing fee for this petition, as it should not have been filed separately from the accounting petition. [LRSF 14.55 B.]

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE LAKISHA WILSON TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #29, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing, merely allowing but not approving the accounting, as no detailed schedule of purchases and changes in the form of assets was filed, as required by Probate Code Section 1063(b), and although a fiduciary, not the attorney for the fiduciary, should sign and verify its own pleadings, including any supplement or amendment to its pleadings. [Probate Code Sect. 1023 and CRC 7.103(c).] Each paper presented for filing must contain two prepunched normal-sized holes, centered 21/2 inches apart and 5/8 inch from the top of the paper. [CRC 2.115.]

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE VIRGINIA N. CACAL special needs TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #3, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Hearing appearance required. Please review Local Rule 14.1 D. for information regarding remote appearances. To appear by Zoom, go to Zoom.com and click Join Meeting, and use the ID and password below. Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Parties appearing should check in for the hearing using their first and last name. 1) Trustee has invested in new ETFs and mutual funds again without prior court approval. Same issue as in account #2. 10/6/2020 Order Approving Second account did not approve trustee having made investments without prior court approval. 2) 10/6/2020 Order Approving Second account awarded Trustee fees of $4,130.00 for services rendered between 12/14/18 and 12/30/19. Joseph Corey alleges this amount was reduced by $90.00 for over payment of fees. This third account shows on 01/09/2020 $4220.00 in fees were paid. It appears trustee overpaid himself $90.00. Trustee is requesting $4,080.00 in fees but has agreed to a reduction of $90.00 to $3,990.00.

rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF RICHARD PATUREL
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #2, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 8/22/22. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: As of January 2008, the Judicial Council form Summary of Account must be used for all conservatorship accounts. [Prob. C. Sect. 2620(a) and CRC 7.575.] The petition caption and prayer do not mention the reduction in bond. A request to reduce bond may be made in the accounting petition, as the accounting should confirm whether or not reduction in bond is appropriate. The prayer also does not set forth the amount of fees requested by the conservator. Original bank statements should be filed confidentially and not attached to a petition. Attorney costs already paid ($3,698.67) mut be itemized. Copies of three invoices showing Federal Express charges without more are not adequate. Please explain the $858.00 disbursement on 8/21/21 described, in part, as "refund due from" Conservator. (It would be helpful if securities were listed in the same (alphabetical) order as in the prior accounting or related accounting. The accounting has not yet been fully reviewed at this time.) Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 8/12/22. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: ** TRANSFERRED TO SOLANO COUNTY** CONSERVATORSHIP OF PATRICIA HOWARD
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Compel Conservatee'S Spouse To Report Community Property; For Reimbursement To Conservator; And To Apply Income And Principal Of Community Property For Support And Maintenance Of Conservatee
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Counsel or parties appearing in person must wear a mask covering their nose and mouth at all times while in the courthouse. Please review LRSF 14.1(D) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF DANIEL MARK POMMON JR
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Mediation
rulings.Rulings: Hearing appearance required. Please review Local Rule 14.1 D. for information regarding remote appearances. To appear by Zoom, go to Zoom.com and click Join Meeting, and use the ID and password below. Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Parties appearing should check in for the hearing using their first and last name. 1) Court to determine how to proceed. 04/13/2022 order referred parties to court's pro bono mediation programs. Mediation was to be completed no later than 6/30/2022. Counsel was to notify the Examiner of the status by 07/05/2022. At prior three hearings counsel indicated an amended petition would be filed but no such amended petition is on file. Whether Probate Code 6124 applies to the Lost Will. 2) Unclear when Decedent died. In PCN-05-287807 Conservatorship of Daniel M. Pommon, Jr the 8th account filed 09/28/2021 alleges Daniel M. Pommon Jr. died on February 14, 2021. The Petition for Probate of Will filed by Janis Rachael Pommon Pozzi alleges Decedent died February 22, 2021. The Petition for Probate of Lost Will filed by Jonathan Campbell alleges Decedent died February 22, 2021. 6/8/2021 Reply to Jonathan Campbell's Objections alleges Decedent died on February 14, 2021. 3) Issues in Janis Rachel Pommon Pozzi's Petition: a. In addition to all persons mentioned in the will, all heirs must be listed even if a decedent died testate. If the decedent had no known heirs, a declaration to that effect must be filed, setting forth the basis for that conclusion and the efforts made to locate heirs. [LRSF 14.18 C. 1)]. b. The proposed order under #2.b. alleges Decedent died on (date): Daniel Mark Pommon Jr. This should be the date of death. Date of death is unclear as there are two dates mentioned in the filings. 4) Issues In Jonathan Campbell's Petition: a. When second generation or more distant heirs are listed, the deceased ancestor(s) through whom they take (or would have taken) must be named, the relationship(s) to the decedent stated, and the date(s) of death specified. [LRSF 14.18 C.] b. Examiner does not have a proposed order. Petitioner must submit a hard copy of the proposed order to the Examiner in room 202 at least five (5) court days in advance of the scheduled hearing date, with the scheduled hearing date noted on the face sheet. [LRSF 14.7].

Pommon Jr. died on February 14, 2021. The Petition for Probate of Will filed by Janis Rachael Pommon Pozzi alleges Decedent died February 22, 2021. The Petition for Probate of Lost Will filed by Jonathan Campbell alleges Decedent died February 22, 2021. 6/8/2021 Reply to Jonathan Campbell's Objections alleges Decedent died on February 14, 2021. 3) Issues in Janis Rachel Pommon Pozzi's Petition: a. In addition to all persons mentioned in the will, all heirs must be listed even if a decedent died testate. If the decedent had no known heirs, a declaration to that effect must be filed, setting forth the basis for that conclusion and the efforts made to locate heirs. [LRSF 14.18 C. 1)]. b. The proposed order under #2.b. alleges Decedent died on (date): Daniel Mark Pommon Jr. This should be the date of death. Date of death is unclear as there are two dates mentioned in the filings. 4) Issues In Jonathan Campbell's Petition: a. When second generation or more distant heirs are listed, the deceased ancestor(s) through whom they take (or would have taken) must be named, the relationship(s) to the decedent stated, and the date(s) of death specified. [LRSF 14.18 C.] b. Examiner does not have a proposed order. Petitioner must submit a hard copy of the proposed order to the Examiner in room 202 at least five (5) court days in advance of the scheduled hearing date, with the scheduled hearing date noted on the face sheet. [LRSF 14.7].

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF DIANA IGNATIUS
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 8/3/22. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: The amendment was file stamped on 6/17/22 but not actually e-filed until 6/22/22, when it was available for viewing. Notice is defective is mailed before the filing (e-filing) of the pleading requiring notice. Please file the seller's closing statement to verify the costs of sale. The costs to be reimbursed to the attorney include the $435 filing fee that was already ordered in the conservatorship case for that final petition. Other costs for "Efiling fee" or "messenger, delivery to Dept. 202" do not indicate what was e-filed or sent by messenger for the Court to determine if they are valid costs. (Refunds for amounts paid during estate administration are not includable in the fee base, nor are amounts like Social Security benefits that were later reclaimed; however, as neither the administrator nor her attorney is requesting extraordinary compensation for selling the real property, the additional $660.37 in "receipts" will be allowed.) Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 7/25/22. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.
